days.name: 30-Day Build Challenge Dashboard
days.what_it_does: Admin tool for tracking 30 builds in 30 days. Logs the build, the post, the numbers, and the human data. Built for Jay and Ian to stay accountable, get the endorphin hits, and prove the system works.
days.who_for: PlainBlack internal - us

days.name: The Smart 404 Page
days.what_it_does: Catches lost visitors instead of bouncing them. Pre-fills a search box from the broken URL, emails the owner the moment a real broken link is hit, and turns the dead end into an on-brand drag-and-drop game with a leaderboard for return visits.
days.who_for: Small business owners running content or service sites where stale links from Google, old social posts, or referrers still send people in — and where bounce rate quietly bleeds traffic they paid to acquire.

days.name: No-Wasted-Quotes Tool
days.what_it_does: A 6-question filter that qualifies trade enquiries before they reach the inbox. Good-fit leads get the quote CTA + an inline contact form. Maybe-fit leads get asked for photos and details first. Bad-fit leads get no contact CTA, just a redirect to a relevant resource. Built around a real Bay of Plenty roofer (Bradley Roofing) so the rules-based logic uses real services, real area, real budget thresholds. Customer-facing live demo on the client subdomain plus a strategic case-study landing page on the main site.
days.who_for: Quote-heavy trade businesses (roofers, plumbers, sparkies, HVAC, builders, landscapers) who pay for leads and lose nights quoting jobs that were never serious.

days.name: Public challenge page
days.what_it_does: plainblackcreative.com/challenge - the live tracker the world can see. Fetches real data from the JSON file.

days.name: Which Service Do I Need? decision tool
days.what_it_does: For a professional services business. User picks their situation, tool directs them to the right service. Removes the "I don't know what to book" friction.

days.name: McIndoe Media CMS · Stage A demo
days.what_it_does: Phone-friendly CMS for McIndoe Media Works that lets Brandon update Work-section images, captions and click URLs without touching code. It opens on the first empty placeholder, previews the change in a local mock of the actual Work section, and stays honest that backend publishing comes next.

days.name: Week 1 Report
days.what_it_does: Blog post + all social. Real numbers from the dashboard. Human check-in included.
days.who_for: PlainBlack / public challenge followers

days.name: Solar Roof-to-Range Tool
days.what_it_does: Solar quote tool for Genr8 Energy (Tasmania). Type your address, trace your roof on satellite, watch panels fill the outline and the savings, payback and system size tick up live. Three exit doors: trace, suburb range, or free site visit.

days.name: "What Should I Order?" - in-store ordering tool
days.what_it_does: In-store QR ordering tool. Photos on every dish, recommender that picks for the customer, allergen filters, smart upsell, second-scan post-meal flow with #ForTheGram + Google review.
days.who_for: Thai, Indian, Chinese, anywhere photos drive the order.

days.name: Blog Generator
days.what_it_does: One topic in, a full content pack out, in the PlainBlack voice. Most AI content sounds like it was written by a very polite fridge: technically correct, structurally tidy, emotionally dead. So we built our own. Outputs: blog post in our voice, Facebook copy, LinkedIn copy, image prompts, alt text, tags. Plus the publishing lifecycle nobody else builds: Draft, Published, Archived (URL stays valid, just unlisted), Deleted (with library tombstone), and one-click Recover from git history. Tag dictionary so the same idea is not three different tags. Voice rules baked into the prompt; banned LinkedInfluencer phrases blocked; Facebook and LinkedIn forced to actually differ.
days.who_for: Internal first: us, to fix our own daily-cadence bottleneck on the 30-day challenge. Productisable for small businesses where 'we should publish more' keeps getting pushed to 'later'. Roofers. Cafes. Accountants. Allied health clinics. Tourism operators. Mechanics. Dog groomers. Consultants. Anyone tired of AI slop and tired of starting every social post from a blank page.

days.name: Why Isn't This Working?
days.what_it_does: A free marketing triage tool for small business owners. Tell it what you tried, what happened, and what you hoped for, and it points at the bit that's probably broken — message, offer-market fit, traffic quality, or missing next step — before you spend more money fixing the wrong thing. Works across websites, ads, video, social, SEO, Google Business Profile, AI tools, and content, whether the effort was paid, DIY, in-house, or organic.
days.who_for: Small business owners in AU/NZ (1 to 15 people, 1 to 7 years in) who've tried websites, ads, video, social, SEO, Google Business Profile, AI tools, or content — paid or DIY — and got unclear or disappointing results. Tired, squeezed, sceptical of vague marketing promises, looking for a straight answer before they spend more.

days.name: Should We Talk Yet?
days.what_it_does: A fit-screener for high-trust, high-deposit service businesses. Five question cards, one at a time, with a fit-pulse that beats faster as the answers stack. Three exit doors: strong fit gets the booking widget; maybe-fit gets a 'things to figure out before talking to ANY photographer' mini-guide; bad-fit gets a respectful redirect to three lower-tier photographers we like (including competitors). No form on the bad-fit door.
days.who_for: High-trust, high-deposit service businesses where bad-fit enquiries cost the most. Wedding photographers, architects, tattoo artists, birth doulas, funeral celebrants.

days.name: The Customer Translator
days.what_it_does: Paste a real customer enquiry. The tool returns four cards: what they actually said, what they actually meant, what they are really worried about, and what to say back. Each card has a confidence score that pulses live. The recommended reply is editable and shippable in one click.
days.who_for: Service businesses drowning in vague, polite, half-formed enquiries. Tradies, clinics, lawyers, designers, accountants.

days.name: Week 2 Audit
days.what_it_does: Numbers from Days 8-14. What almost killed us. What surprised us. First cut of 'Best & Worst AI Chats': Claude (handsome, wise, beloved) vs GPT (well-meaning but not invited to dinner).

days.name: The Verdict Card
days.what_it_does: Make, model, year, symptom, the quote they were given. The tool pulls live used-car valuations and runs the call: fix it, fix-and-flip-it, drive it till it dies, or get out now. Each verdict comes with a one-line plain-English summary. The mechanic's logo is on the result. Customers text the verdict to their partner.
days.who_for: Independent mechanics with strong reviews and no franchise overlords. Roll the same engine to HVAC, appliance repair, roofing, fencing, marine.

days.name: The Objection Wall
days.what_it_does: A public page on a clinic's site that lists the five real objections their front desk hears every week, with the clinic's honest answer underneath. Pulled from actual customer messages (anonymised). One objection is 'I tried physio before and it didn't work.' The clinic answers it like a grown-up. No marketing voice.
days.who_for: Allied health clinics (osteo, physio, chiro), dentists, and any high-trust local service where the website hides the practitioners.
days.client: Tauranga / Hamilton osteo or physio clinic (TBC)

days.name: The Voice Twin
days.what_it_does: Custom voice profiles you can use in any AI tool. Upload or paste five to ten samples of your own writing. The tool extracts signature moves, banned phrases, sentence rhythm, hedge habits. Output: a portable Voice Card (interactive page at plainblackcreative.com/voice/[slug]) you can drop into ChatGPT, Claude, the PB blog-gen, anywhere. Part 1 of the four-build Voice Stack arc that ends with Ain.
days.who_for: Founders, copywriters, and small business owners who want their content (or their team's content) written in one calibrated voice instead of three different ones.

days.name: The First Seven Days
days.what_it_does: An interactive timeline of the first week after a death in the family. Drag through it. Each stage shows what the family does, what the funeral director does, what most people worry about, what most people forget, and a realistic cost band. No upsells. No 'packages'. It is information given freely to people who need it most.
days.who_for: Independent funeral directors, will and estate lawyers, hospices, aged-care providers, immigration consultants. Anywhere the customer is anxious, the process is opaque, and trust is the whole game.
days.client: Tauranga / Napier / Nelson independent funeral director (TBC)

days.name: The Playbook Spawner
days.what_it_does: Three quick inputs: business type, current biggest problem, what they have tried. The tool picks the right playbook from the library, then rewrites every example, every template, every checklist with the user's specifics swapped in. Output: an interactive personalised playbook hosted at plainblackcreative.com/playbooks/[slug]. AI-powered. Never stale. The web version of hiring PlainBlack, at the same URL forever.
days.who_for: Small business owners who would benefit from a $99 playbook but want to see a tailored sample first.
days.client: Demo on a real Tauranga cafe and a real Hamilton trade business

days.name: Get A Brief, Not A Quote
days.what_it_does: The V3 schema, productised, public, no walls. Ten sections that play like a guided story, not a form. AI co-pilot in the margin paraphrasing what it heard back. Live completeness gauge. Final output: a live brief page at plainblackcreative.com/briefs/[slug] that the user can send to any agency on earth. Including agencies that are not us. Especially agencies that are not us.
days.who_for: Small business owners about to engage an agency (any agency) who do not know what to ask for. Every founder who has ever been ghosted after sending a vague 'can you quote us a website' email.

days.name: Week 3 Audit
days.what_it_does: Pipeline as a count, not as case studies. Discretion as a flex. The arc starts to surface (Voice Twin shipped, Voice Fingerprint inbound). Round two of 'Best & Worst AI Chats': GPT confidently misquotes the brief; Claude remembers it word for word.

days.name: The Fix First
days.what_it_does: The diagnostic that does not recommend a service. It assigns an identity. Six cards, one at a time, with a live diagnosis confidence gauge. Final screen assigns an identity (Controller / Delegator / Stuck Between) with a path. The 'Stuck Between' path offers a 12-minute call with a pre-filled agenda drafted from the user's answers, no pitch.
days.who_for: The front door for the whole PB site. Every small business owner who has ever wondered 'what should I fix first?' and got conflicting advice from three different agencies.

days.name: The Voice Signature Mural
days.what_it_does: Pull a cafe's last 200 Google reviews via API. Run them through a typographic generator. Output a Voice Signature Mural: a poster made of the actual words customers use about the cafe, weighted by frequency. Then print it as a 2m vinyl, shoot a 30-second video installing it on the cafe's wall, post the whole thing.
days.who_for: Hospitality, retail, gyms, hotels, dental practices. Anywhere with strong character, a customer base, and at least one blank wall.

days.name: The Slow Week Engine
days.what_it_does: Input the trade and location. The tool pulls the live 14-day MetService forecast. Detects dry windows. Generates a Slow Week Special campaign for those windows: SMS to past customers, Google Business Profile post, Facebook post, and a one-page landing page we host on a free subdomain, publishable in two clicks.
days.who_for: Trades whose work depends on weather (landscapers, arborists, painters, roofers) and tour operators heading into shoulder season. Anyone watching a slow stretch wondering how to fill it.
days.client: Hawke's Bay / Tauranga landscaper or Queenstown tour operator (TBC)

days.name: The Calendar That Shakes
days.what_it_does: A draggable month grid that fills itself. Each cell is a real post: hook, draft caption, image direction, CTA, business goal, difficulty rating, and one line that says 'this post exists because…' so the user learns the strategy as they read. Hit 'shake the calendar' to re-roll only the slots they don't like. One-click handoff to Day 10's blog generator turns any cell into a finished PB-voice draft.
days.who_for: Small businesses who know they should post but don't. Butchers, cafes, trades, clinics, consultants.

days.name: The Voice Fingerprint
days.what_it_does: Paste a URL or copy. The tool runs four passes side by side. 1. The Voice Fingerprint: radial chart of jargon, hedge words, generic phrases. 2. The Slop Detector: flags keyword-stuffed copy. 3. The Technical Audit: alt tags, H1, schema, Lighthouse. 4. The Hypocrisy Check: same audit on the agency's own client sites.
days.who_for: Small business owners auditing their own copy, or auditing the agencies they are considering. Plus: a public roast of three local agencies whose websites sound like printer manuals wearing cologne.
days.client: Demoed on three local agencies (logos blurred for launch)

days.name: The Agency Audit
days.what_it_does: Two tools, one URL, every other agency exposed. Tool 1 (Quote Decoder): paste an agency proposal, get the translation, with specific bullshit deliverables called out by name. Tool 2 (Versus Table): the PlainBlack column updates dynamically with what the user actually needs; the Everyone Else column stays frozen no matter what the user inputs (Monthly Retainer / Increase Ad Spend / Monthly Report / Bonus: Free Zoom Call).

days.name: Week 4 Audit
days.what_it_does: Two days from the capstone. Numbers, energy, what we are saving for last. No spoilers. Final round of 'Best & Worst AI Chats': Claude prepares the slide deck; GPT is still arguing about whether the slide deck was a good idea.

days.name: The Founder Script
days.what_it_does: Three inputs: your business, what you did this week, what you want viewers to do next. The tool generates a 60- to 90-second script in your voice (pulled from your Voice Twin from Day 17), with stage directions, b-roll suggestions, and a recommended hook in the first three seconds. Five script variants per generation. Output: a teleprompter-ready script page at plainblackcreative.com/scripts/[slug] (or JSON payload ready for the Day 30 build).

days.name: Today I Built… Ain
days.what_it_does: Capstone. A custom HeyGen avatar called Ain (AI + Ian), trained on real footage of Ian, calibrated to a slightly exaggerated comic register. Voice cloned from the Day 17 Voice Twin. Script written by the Day 29 Founder Script generator. Ain delivers a 90-second over-the-top tribute to Jay's brilliance. Productised service: AI Twin by PlainBlack.

weekly_reports.whats_working: The challenge is forcing output. Six builds shipped, six posts published, and the public tracker is keeping us accountable. The biggest surprise is that the systems we built to run the challenge — tracker, blog generator, posting workflow and reporting loop - may be some of the most useful tools we can eventually package for businesses.
weekly_reports.what_to_change: The build → blog → post → tracker loop is still too disconnected. Week 2 stays on the planned builds, but the process needs to be smoother so each tool is easier to capture, explain, publish and track without losing half the day to admin.
